title: Email Sequence Summary
description: Structured metadata for an email sequence produced by /email-sequences. Contains sequence type, email count, subject lines, timing, and CTA data. Designed for downstream automation -- feeding sequence data to an ESP (Mailchimp, ConvertKit, HubSpot) for automated deployment.

sequence_name.description: Human-readable name for this sequence, e.g. 'Cold Email Kit Welcome Sequence'.

email_count.description: Total number of emails in the sequence.

created_date.description: ISO 8601 date when this sequence was created.

created_by.description: The skill that created this sequence, typically '/email-sequences'.

campaign_ref.description: Path to the campaign directory this sequence belongs to, e.g. './campaigns/cold-email-kit-welcome/'.

trigger.description: What triggers the sequence to start for a subscriber.

event.description: The triggering event, e.g. 'lead magnet download', 'purchase', 'tag added', 'list signup'.

source.description: Where the subscriber comes from, e.g. 'landing page', 'blog opt-in', 'checkout'.

segment.description: Audience segment this sequence targets, e.g. 'new subscribers', 'trial users', 'past customers'.

total_duration_days.description: Total duration of the sequence from first email to last, in days.

voice_profile_ref.description: Path to the voice profile used, typically './brand/voice-profile.md'.

positioning_angle.description: The positioning angle driving this sequence, e.g. 'The Anti-Course Course'.

emails.description: Metadata for each email in the sequence, in send order.

purpose.description: The strategic purpose of this email, e.g. 'Deliver lead magnet', 'Build trust with proof', 'Overcome objections'.

subject_lines.description: Subject line variants for A/B testing. First is the recommended winner.

variant_label.description: Label for this variant, e.g. 'A', 'B', 'C' or 'curiosity', 'proof', 'direct'.

rationale.description: Why this subject line should work, e.g. 'Curiosity gap + specificity'.

send_day.description: Day to send relative to sequence start (Day 0 = trigger day).

send_time.description: Recommended send time, e.g. '7:00 AM', '10:00 AM local time'.

text.description: CTA button or link text, e.g. 'Download the Kit', 'Read the Case Study'.

url_placeholder.description: Placeholder URL for the CTA destination, e.g. '{lead_magnet_url}', '{case_study_url}'.

type.description: Type of action the CTA drives.

file_path.description: Relative path to the full email file, e.g. './campaigns/welcome/emails/01-delivery.md'.

estimated_word_count.description: Approximate word count of the email body.

tags_to_apply.description: Subscriber tags to apply when this email is opened or clicked. For ESP automation.

performance_targets.description: Expected performance benchmarks for this sequence type.

open_rate.description: Target open rate, e.g. '40-55%' for welcome sequences.

conversion_rate.description: Target conversion rate for the sequence goal, e.g. '3-5% to paid'.

unsubscribe_rate.description: Acceptable unsubscribe rate, e.g. 'Under 1% per email'.

esp_compatibility.description: Email service providers this sequence has been formatted for.

items.description: ESP name or 'generic' for platform-agnostic format.
